name: Palladium
description: The Palladium was an ancient wooden statue of Pallas Athena, believed to guarantee the safety of the city that possessed it. Legend states it fell from the heavens, sent by Zeus himself. It was said to have been originally located in Troy, ensuring the city's invincibility as long as it remained within its walls. The theft of the Palladium by Odysseus and Diomedes during the Trojan War is a crucial event, believed to have been necessary for the Greeks to finally conquer Troy. Accounts differ on how the Palladium was obtained, some claiming it was taken by stealth, others that it was willingly given up by a Trojan priestess. After the fall of Troy, the Palladium's journey is shrouded in further mystery, with various cities claiming to possess the genuine artifact. Roman tradition held that Aeneas brought the Palladium to Italy, where it was kept in the Temple of Vesta in Rome, safeguarding the Roman state. Its continued existence was seen as vital to Rome's power and prosperity, and its loss would have been considered a catastrophic omen. The Palladium became a symbol of divine protection and the enduring power of a city or nation.
subtitle: Guardian of cities, fallen from heaven
origin: Fallen from the heavens (sent by Zeus)

keyMyths.title: The Theft of the Palladium
keyMyths.description: Odysseus and Diomedes bravely infiltrated Troy to steal the Palladium, knowing its presence ensured the city's safety. This act was crucial in allowing the Greeks to eventually win the Trojan War.

keyMyths.title: Palladium in Rome
keyMyths.description: According to Roman tradition, Aeneas carried the Palladium from Troy to Italy, eventually placing it in the Temple of Vesta. It was considered a sacred object that guaranteed Rome's safety and prosperity.

sources.author: Homer
sources.description: Mentions the importance of the Palladium to Troy's safety.
sources.text: Mentions the importance of the Palladium to Troy's safety.

sources.author: Virgil
sources.description: Describes Aeneas bringing the Palladium to Italy.
sources.text: Describes Aeneas bringing the Palladium to Italy.

sources.author: Apollodorus
sources.description: Provides an account of the theft of the Palladium.
